title: How I Approach New Product Builds
excerpt: A practical framework I use to move from idea to reliable production delivery with fewer surprises.
body: Most projects fail in the handoff between planning and execution. I start by reducing ambiguity with a lightweight discovery checklist.
Then I break delivery into vertical slices that can ship independently. This helps me validate quickly and limit rework.
Finally, I define quality gates early: accessibility checks, performance budgets, and monitoring from day one.

title: A Maintainable React Architecture for Solo Builders
excerpt: A simple but scalable folder and data strategy for personal projects that need to grow over time.
body: The key is separating content, UI, and routing from the beginning. It prevents tight coupling and keeps changes local.
For content-heavy sites, data-driven rendering lets you add sections with minimal code edits and fewer regressions.
Even on a small project, typed content models save time because they reveal missing fields early.

title: Performance Rules I Actually Use on Real Projects
excerpt: A concise checklist for building fast interfaces without over-optimizing too early.
body: I begin with image discipline, route-level code splitting, and sensible caching before touching advanced techniques.
I track core metrics after launch, then optimize based on evidence rather than assumptions.
Sustainable performance work is iterative and tied to user outcomes, not isolated benchmark numbers.
